title.en: Moving States with Your Vehicle — NOC, Re-Registration, Road Tax Refund and the BH Series

answerBox.en: Keeping a vehicle in another state beyond 12 months requires re-registration: take an NOC (Form 28) from your origin RTO, pay the new state's road tax, and apply for a new registration mark — then claim the origin state's pro-rata road tax refund. Most steps run on parivahan.gov.in. Frequently-transferred employees should consider the BH (Bharat) series, which removes the whole cycle.

en.label: Use in new state on old plates

en.title: NOC first: Form 28 from the origin RTO
en.detail: Apply for the **NOC (Form 28**, in triplicate) at the RTO where the vehicle is registered — online on [parivahan.gov.in](https://parivahan.gov.in) in most states. The RTO verifies **no pending challans/taxes and no theft record** (CCTNS check), and a financed vehicle needs the **financier's consent**. The NOC notes the destination state. Validity is limited (commonly 6 months) — sequence your move accordingly.

en.title: Pay the new state's road tax + re-register
en.summary: Form 20/27, address proof, tax on depreciated value.
en.detail: In the new state: pay **road tax** (lifetime tax computed on the invoice value less depreciation — states differ meaningfully in rates), then apply for **assignment of a new registration mark (Forms 27/20)** with the NOC, RC, insurance, PUC, address proof and fees; fitness/inspection where required. Update the **insurance policy's registration details and address** — premiums and validity follow the vehicle's state. Hypothecation carries over onto the new RC.

en.title: Claim the origin state's refund
en.summary: Pro-rata lifetime tax comes back — most people forget.
en.detail: After re-registration, claim the **pro-rata refund of lifetime road tax** from the origin state (application with the new RC, old RC/tax receipts, NOC copy — Form 16/state-specific). Refunds run on the unexpired-years proportion and take months; they are nonetheless real money on a car taxed at 8–15% of invoice value. File it — the state will not volunteer it.

en.title: Or skip the cycle: the BH series
en.summary: For transferable-job holders — one mark, pan-India.
en.detail: Eligible owners — **central/state government employees and private-sector employees whose companies have offices in 4+ states/UTs** — can register new vehicles in the **BH (Bharat) series**: road tax paid in 2-year blocks, no re-registration on interstate moves. Existing-vehicle conversion to BH has been enabled progressively (check current rules on parivahan). For serially-transferred families, BH removes this guide's entire problem — choose it at purchase.

rows.label: New-state road tax
rows.note: On depreciated value; the big number

rows.label: Refund recovered from origin state
rows.note: File for it — months, but real money

en.footnote: Skipping re-registration invites seizure/penalties in the new state and complicates insurance claims — the 12-month grace is generous; use it to finish the paperwork, not to hide.

en: car re registration another state process
road tax refund on vehicle transfer
dusre state me gaadi register kaise kare

en.q: Do I really need to re-register if I keep my old plates?
en.a: Beyond 12 months of keeping the vehicle in the new state, yes (Section 47, MV Act) — enforcement varies but road-tax evasion penalties, checkpost seizures and insurance-claim friction all bite the overstayer. Students and genuinely temporary postings within the window are fine on old plates.

en.q: Can I do the whole process without traveling back to the origin state?
en.a: Mostly yes: Form 28 NOC applications are online in most states, and documents move by post/representative. The refund claim is also by application. One practical dependency is clearing old challans — do that on the portal before applying, or the NOC stalls.

en.q: How is the new state's road tax calculated on my used car?
en.a: On the original invoice value with age-based depreciation per that state's schedule, at that state's rate (roughly 6–15% band across states, higher for costlier vehicles/diesel in some). The refund from the origin state offsets part of it — always net the two before deciding whether re-registration or selling-and-rebuying makes sense.

en.q: Who can take a BH series registration?
en.a: Government employees (centre/state/PSU) and private employees of companies with offices in four or more states/UTs — proof via employment certificate (Form 60). Tax runs in 2-year blocks at BH rates; the mark stays valid across India with no NOC/re-registration on moves.

en.q: What about my driving licence when I move states?
en.a: Your DL is valid pan-India — no re-issue required. You may optionally update the address (helps with local processes); that is a simple application with address proof, doable online in most states via parivahan.

reviewer: Adv. Arjun Nair, Bar Council of Kerala, KER/0973/2012
